docs.title: Fungoid Theme
docs.text: A dark, cozy terminal theme for iTerm, Oh My Zsh, and VS Code.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• 🌙 Deep, cozy dark colors inspired by fungal networks
  • 🎨 Consistent color palette across all your tools
  • ⚡ Optimized for coding and terminal work
  • 🎯 Carefully tuned contrast ratios

docs.title: Install Oh My Zsh Theme
docs.text:
# Copy the theme to your Oh My Zsh custom themes directory
cp oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/fungoid.zsh-theme $ZSH_CUSTOM/themes/

# Enable it in ~/.zshrc
ZSH_THEME="fungoid"

docs.title: Install iTerm Colors
docs.text:
# Double-click the .itermcolors file to import
open iterm/Fungoid.itermcolors

docs.title: Install VS Code Theme
docs.text:
# Open VS Code, go to Extensions, and install from the VS Code Marketplace
# Or manually install from vscode/fungoid-vscode-theme/

docs.title: Color Palette
docs.text: See COLORS.md for the full color reference.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Installation Guide

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to Fungoid will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• CONTRIBUTING.md with contribution guidelines
  • Screenshots folder for visual documentation
  • Color palette reference in COLORS.md
  • Expanded README with installation guides and feature highlights

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Optimized VSCode theme file (61% size reduction)
  • Enhanced documentation with comprehensive guides

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text: • Syntax highlighting color consistency across file types

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Initial release of Fungoid theme
  • iTerm2 color scheme
  • Oh My Zsh theme
  • VSCode theme extension
  • Dark theme with orange (#ff7800) and green (#3cdc50) as primary accents
  • Dracula-inspired syntax highlighting
  • Zsh prompt with timestamp, path, git status, and exit codes
  • Syntax highlighting for strings, comments, keywords, types, operators
  • LICENSE (MIT)
  • README.md with installation instructions
  • INSTALL.md with detailed setup guides

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Refined color palette for better readability
  • Improved punctuation and comment colors (muted grey #a09080)
  • Consolidated duplicate scope definitions in VSCode theme

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• String value coloring (now properly green)
  • Comment and punctuation visibility

docs.title: Fungoid Color Palette
docs.text: Complete reference of all colors used in the Fungoid theme across all platforms.

docs.title: Primary Colors
docs.text: Name Hex RGB Usage Primary Orange #ff7800 rgb(255, 120, 0) Prompts, keywords, highlights, user@host Primary Green #3cdc50 rgb(60, 220, 80) Timestamps, success states, strings

docs.title: Secondary Colors
docs.text: Name Hex RGB Usage Pink #d966ff rgb(217, 102, 255) Types, classes, function names Cyan #00d296 rgb(0, 210, 150) Accents, decorations, git staged Purple #a855f7 rgb(168, 85, 247) Operators, special syntax Muted Grey #a09080 rgb(160, 144, 128) Comments, punctuation, paths, meta Yellow #ffbe00 rgb(255, 190, 0) Secondary highlights, warnings Red #ff5a5a rgb(255, 90, 90) Errors, warnings, exit codes, git untracked

docs.title: Base Colors
docs.text: Name Hex RGB Usage Background #1e1e1e rgb(30, 30, 30) Editor/terminal background Foreground #e8e6e3 rgb(232, 230, 227) Primary text, default foreground

docs.title: Git Status Colors (Zsh)
docs.text: State Color Hex Staged Cyan #00d296 Modified Orange #ff7800 Untracked Red #ff5a5a Branch Muted Grey #a09080

docs.title: Consistency Across Platforms
docs.text:
All three theme variants (iTerm, Zsh, VSCode) use the same hex values to ensure visual consistency:

  • iTerm2: iterm/Fungoid.itermcolors
  • Oh My Zsh: oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/fungoid.zsh-theme
  • VSCode: vscode/fungoid-vscode-theme/themes/fungoid.json

docs.title: Orange (#ff7800)
docs.text:
• High visibility against dark background
  • Warm, energetic feel
  • Distinct from other common terminal colors
  • Used for: Primary prompts, keywords, attention-grabbing elements

docs.title: Green (#3cdc50)
docs.text:
• Natural association with success/safety
  • Bright enough to stand out
  • Used for: Timestamps, success messages, string literals

docs.title: Pink (#d966ff)
docs.text:
• Complementary to green (Dracula-inspired)
  • Good for type differentiation
  • Used for: Types, classes, constructors

docs.title: Cyan (#00d296)
docs.text:
• Cool balance to warm orange
  • Git staged indicator
  • Used for: Accents, decorations

docs.title: Purple (#a855f7)
docs.text:
• Operator differentiation
  • Adds visual variety
  • Used for: Operators, special syntax

docs.title: Muted Grey (#a09080)
docs.text:
• Reduced eye strain for non-critical text
  • Better than pure grey for long sessions
  • Used for: Comments, paths, metadata

docs.title: VSCode Test
docs.text: Open any code file and verify: - String literals → Green - Keywords (if, else, return) → Orange - Comments → Muted Grey - Types/Classes → Pink - Operators (+, -, =) → Purple

docs.title: Accessibility
docs.text:
• All foreground colors pass WCAG AA contrast ratio against #1e1e1e background
  • Orange and green provide distinct color-blind friendly differentiation
  • Muted grey reduces eye strain during extended coding sessions

docs.title: Contributing New Colors
docs.text:
If you want to suggest color adjustments: 1. Open an issue with your proposal 2. Include before/after screenshots 3. Explain the use case 4. Provide hex/RGB values

See CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines.

docs.title: Contributing to Fungoid
docs.text: Thanks for your interest! Here's how you can help.

docs.title: Reporting Bugs
docs.text:
Before creating bug reports, please check the existing issues to avoid duplicates. When creating a bug report, include as much detail as possible:

  • Use a clear and descriptive title
  • Describe the exact steps to reproduce the problem
  • Provide specific examples to demonstrate the steps
  • Describe the behavior you observed after following the steps
  • Explain which behavior you expected to see instead
  • Include screenshots if applicable
  • Specify your environment (OS, terminal version, shell version)

docs.title: Suggesting Enhancements
docs.text:
Enhancement suggestions are tracked as GitHub issues. When creating an enhancement suggestion:

  • Use a clear and descriptive title
  • Provide a step-by-step description of the suggested enhancement
  • Provide specific examples to demonstrate the steps
  • Describe the current behavior and the expected behavior
  • Explain why this enhancement would be useful
  • List some other themes or applications where this feature exists

docs.title: Pull Requests
docs.text:
• Fill in the required template
  • Do not include issue numbers in the PR title
  • Include screenshots and animated GIFs of your changes
  • Follow the existing style and conventions
  • Document new features in README.md
  • Test changes across all supported platforms (macOS, Linux)
  • Write a clear and descriptive PR description

docs.title: VSCode Theme Changes
docs.text:
1. Edit vscode/fungoid-vscode-theme/themes/fungoid.json
  2. Test in VSCode: code --install-extension path/to/fungoid-theme-1.0.0.vsix
  3. Verify syntax highlighting across multiple file types
  4. Run vsce package to build the extension
  5. Update CHANGELOG.md

docs.title: iTerm Theme Changes
docs.text:
1. Edit iterm/Fungoid.itermcolors
  2. Import into iTerm2 Preferences > Profiles > Colors
  3. Test with various commands and git status
  4. Update CHANGELOG.md

docs.title: Zsh Theme Changes
docs.text:
1. Edit oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/fungoid.zsh-theme
  2. Copy to ~/.oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/
  3. Set ZSH_THEME="fungoid" in ~/.zshrc
  4. Test prompt with various git states and commands
  5. Update CHANGELOG.md

docs.title: Color Naming
docs.text:
• Use semantic names (e.g., primaryOrange, accentGreen, mutedGrey)
  • Keep hex codes consistent across all theme files
  • Document new colors in COLORS.md

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• Use lowercase for color values in JSON
  • Maintain consistent indentation (2 spaces)
  • Keep related scopes grouped together
  • Avoid duplicate scope definitions

docs.title: Questions?
docs.text: Feel free to open an issue for questions or concerns.

docs.title: Install Fungoid Theme
docs.text: Complete installation guides for all platforms.

docs.title: Method 1: Direct Download
docs.text:
1. Download the theme file: 

    curl -o ~/Downloads/Fungoid.itermcolors https://raw.githubusercontent.com/marzukia/fungoid/main/iterm/Fungoid.itermcolors

  2. Open iTerm2 Preferences (Cmd + ,)

  3. Navigate to Profiles > Colors
  4. Click on "Color Presets..." dropdown at the bottom
  5. Select "Import..."
  6. Choose the downloaded Fungoid.itermcolors file
  7. Select your profile and apply the theme from the presets dropdown

docs.title: Method 2: Manual Import
docs.text:
1. Clone or download the repository
  2. Open iTerm2 Preferences (Cmd + ,)
  3. Navigate to Profiles > Colors
  4. Click "Color Presets..." > "Import..."
  5. Select iterm/Fungoid.itermcolors from the repository
  6. Apply to your profile

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
Open a new terminal tab and run: 

echo -e "\033[38;2;255;120;0mOrange Text\033[0m\n\033[38;2;60;220;80mGreen Text\033[0m"

You should see orange and green text.

docs.title: Method 1: Clone to Custom Themes (Recommended)
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository: 

    git clone https://github.com/marzukia/fungoid.git ~/.oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/fungoid

  2. Edit your ~/.zshrc file: 

    nano ~/.zshrc

  3. Find the ZSH_THEME line and change it to: 

    ZSH_THEME="fungoid"

  4. Save and exit (Ctrl+X, then Y, then Enter)

  5. Reload your shell: 

    exec zsh

docs.title: Method 2: Copy Theme File Directly
docs.text:
1. Download the theme file: 

    curl -o ~/.oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/fungoid.zsh-theme https://raw.githubusercontent.com/marzukia/fungoid/main/oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/fungoid.zsh-theme

  2. Edit your ~/.zshrc: 

    ZSH_THEME="fungoid"

  3. Reload your shell: 

    exec zsh

docs.title: Method 3: Manual Installation
docs.text:
1. Copy the theme file: 

    cp oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/fungoid.zsh-theme ~/.oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/

  2. Edit ~/.zshrc: 

    ZSH_THEME="fungoid"

  3. Apply: 

    source ~/.zshrc

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
Your prompt should now show: - Green timestamp (HH:MM:SS) - Orange user@host - Muted grey path with git branch - Colored git status indicators

Test with: 

cd /tmp
git status
ls -la

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Prompt looks broken or shows raw codes: - Install a Nerd Font: https://www.nerdfonts.com/ - Set your terminal font to the Nerd Font

Git status not showing: - Ensure you're in a git repository - Check that plugin=(git) is in your ~/.zshrc

Colors look wrong: - Ensure iTerm2 is using the Fungoid color scheme - Check that your terminal is set to 256-color or true color mode

docs.title: Method 1: VSIX Installation (Recommended)
docs.text:
1. Download the latest .vsix file from the releases page

  2. Install via Command Palette:

  3. Press Ctrl+Shift+P (or Cmd+Shift+P on Mac)
  4. Type "Extensions: Install from VSIX"

  5. Select the downloaded .vsix file

  6. Activate the theme:

  7. Press Ctrl+K Ctrl+T (or Cmd+K Ctrl+T)
  8. Select "Fungoid"

docs.title: Method 2: Manual Installation
docs.text:
1. Copy the fungoid-vscode-theme folder to your VSCode extensions directory:
  2. Linux: ~/.vscode/extensions/
  3. macOS: ~/.vscode/extensions/

  4. Windows: %USERPROFILE%\.vscode\extensions\

  5. Reload VSCode

  6. Activate: Ctrl+K Ctrl+T > "Fungoid"

docs.title: Method 3: Development Installation
docs.text:
1. Navigate to the theme directory: 

    cd vscode/fungoid-vscode-theme

  2. Install VSCE (VSCode Extension Manager): 

    npm install -g @vscode/vsce

  3. Package the extension: 

    vsce package

  4. Install the generated .vsix file: 

    code --install-extension fungoid-theme-1.0.0.vsix

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
1. Open any code file (JavaScript, Python, Rust, etc.)
  2. Check that syntax highlighting matches the theme colors
  3. Verify comments are muted grey and strings are green

docs.title: Configure as Default
docs.text:
Add to your settings.json: 

{
  "workbench.colorTheme": "Fungoid"
}

docs.title: Color Scheme Reference
docs.text: After installation, verify your colors match:

docs.title: Zsh
docs.text:
# Test prompt elements
echo $USER@$HOST  # Should be orange
pwd               # Should be muted grey

docs.title: VSCode
docs.text: Open a JavaScript file and check: - Strings → Green - Keywords → Orange - Comments → Muted Grey - Types → Pink - Operators → Purple

docs.title: iTerm2
docs.text:
1. Open iTerm2 Preferences > Profiles > Colors
  2. Change "Color Presets..." to a different theme
  3. To remove: delete Fungoid.itermcolors from your imports

docs.title: Oh My Zsh
docs.text:
1. Edit ~/.zshrc and change ZSH_THEME to another theme (e.g., robbyrussell)
  2. Remove the theme files: 
    rm -rf ~/.oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/fungoid

docs.title: VSCode
docs.text:
1. Ctrl+Shift+P > "Extensions: Show Installed Extensions"
  2. Find "Fungoid Theme"
  3. Click "Uninstall"
  4. Reload VSCode

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Check out the README.md for color palette and features
  • See CONTRIBUTING.md to help improve the theme
  • View CHANGELOG.md for version history

docs.title: 🍄 Fungoid Channel Setup
docs.text:
**multi-channel logging for the modern dev** ![Discord](https://img.shields.io/badge/Discord-%235865F2.svg?style=for-the-badge&logo=discord&logoColor=white) ![Telegram](https://img.shields.io/badge/Telegram-%230088CC.svg?style=for-the-badge&logo=telegram&logoColor=white) ![Nostr](https://img.shields.io/badge/Nostr-%233cdc50.svg?style=for-the-badge&logo=nostr&logoColor=white)

fungoid supports discord, telegram, and nostr for logging and interaction.

docs.title: 🎨 Theme Colors
docs.text:
all channel output uses the fungoid color palette:

# orange #ff7800
echo -e "\033[38;2;255;120;0m→\033[0m discord"
# green #3cdc50
echo -e "\033[38;2;60;220;80m✓\033[0m telegram"
# pink #d966ff
echo -e "\033[38;2;217;102;255m◉\033[0m nostr"

full color reference: COLORS.md

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Create a Discord Bot
  2. Go to Discord Developer Portal
  3. Create a new application
  4. Go to "Bot" section and create a bot

  5. Copy the bot token

  6. Add Bot to Your Server

  7. Go to "OAuth2" > "URL Generator"
  8. Select scopes: bot
  9. Select permissions: Send Messages, Read Message History

  10. Copy the generated URL and invite the bot

  11. Configure Fungoid 

    export DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN="your-bot-token"
export DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID="your-channel-id"

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: the bot will log messages and can respond to commands in the configured channel.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Create a Bot
  2. Message @BotFather on Telegram
  3. Send /newbot and follow the instructions

  4. Copy the bot token

  5. Get Your Chat ID

  6. Message your bot
  7. Visit https://api.telegram.org/bot<YOUR_TOKEN>/getUpdates

  8. Find your chat ID in the response

  9. Configure Fungoid 

    export TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN="your-bot-token"
export TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID="your-chat-id"

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: send messages to your bot and it will log them to the configured chat.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Generate Keys 

    # Using nostr-cli or any nostr wallet
nostr-keygen

  2. Configure Fungoid 

    export NOSTR_PRIVATE_KEY="your-nsec"
export NOSTR_RELAY="wss://relay.damus.io"

  3. Optional: Publish Public Key

  4. Share your npub so others can message you

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: messages are logged to the nostr network via the configured relay.

docs.title: Multi-Channel
docs.text:
you can enable multiple channels simultaneously. messages will be logged to all active channels.

# Enable all channels
export DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN="..."
export DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID="..."
export TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN="..."
export TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID="..."
export NOSTR_PRIVATE_KEY="..."
export NOSTR_RELAY="wss://relay.damus.io"

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• never commit tokens to git
  • use environment variables or a .env file (add to .gitignore)
  • rotate tokens regularly
  • use separate bots for development and production

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
discord bot not responding: - check bot permissions in server settings - verify the channel ID is correct - ensure bot token is valid

telegram messages not sending: - bot must be started (send any message to it first) - chat ID must be correct - check network connectivity

nostr not publishing: - verify private key format (nsec) - check relay is reachable - ensure network connectivity
